qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated cron job execution allows unauthorized administrative task triggering
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the unauthenticated Cron controller, not the REST API sort parameter ORDER BY SQL injection in ModelClass::getOrderBy() or DbQuery::orderBy().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in Myfiles controller allows arbitrary file read via URL manipulation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets Myfiles path concatenation and file reads, not the API sort parameter reaching an unsafe SQL ORDER BY clause.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PHP Object Injection via unserialize() in WidgetSelect allows potential remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets WidgetSelect::processFormData() unserialize() handling, not the REST API sort ORDER BY SQL construction flaw.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL injection in NextCode::get() and ModelClass::newCode() via unsanitized table/column parameters
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same repository and broad vulnerability class, but the final finding identifies NextCode::get() and ModelClass::newCode() identifier concatenation, not the advisory's API sort parameter flow through ModelClass::getOrderBy() or DbQuery::orderBy().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in Http class allows internal network access and cloud metadata retrieval
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unrestricted URL fetching in the Http class, not SQL injection in API ORDER BY sort handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: authenticated REST API users controlling the sort parameter so that ModelClass::all() reaches ModelClass::getOrderBy(), or DbQuery::orderBy(), and concatenates attacker-controlled ORDER BY expressions. The only final SQL injection finding is a distinct identifier-concatenation issue in NextCode::get() and ModelClass::newCode().
evidence: Advisory GHSA-cjfx-qhwm-hf99 describes SQL injection in FacturaScripts REST API sort handling, specifically Core/Model/Base/ModelClass.php::getOrderBy() and Core/DbQuery.php::orderBy().

sql-injection-nextcode/vulnerability_report.json names Core/NextCode.php:56 and Core/Template/ModelClass.php:502 as the vulnerable locations and describes table/column parameters reaching SELECT MAX(...), not API sort parameters reaching ORDER BY.

The remaining qualifying findings are authorization bypass, path traversal, PHP object injection, and SSRF; none share the advisory's vulnerable function, endpoint, parser state, missing guard, or source-to-sink path.
